(916) 322·5662 
322·5660 
March 20, 1985 
Afton Crooks 
Conflict of Interest Coordinator 
University of California 
2200 University Avenue, Room 349 
Berkeley, CA 
94720 
322-6'«1 
322-6444 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-85-070 
Dear Ms. Crooks: 
This letter will formally confirm my previous telephone 
advice to the effect that you may treat the disclosure category 
for the Senior Vice Chancellor-Administration, San Francisco 
Campus as being 4 and 17, rather than 1. 
The assignment of 
category 1 was a clerical error when your office rearranged the 
order of designated officials and resulted in one campus' 
official being treated differently than like officials on all 
other campuses. 
This would be inappropriate. 
Therefore we will 
treat the category as being 4 and 17 for this annual report and 
will correct the clerical error when the Code is revised next 
fall. 
REL:plh 
Sincerely, 
Robert E. Leidigh 
Counsel 
Legal Division 


UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA 
DA\'lD PIERPO,\T GARD,\ER 
HO,\ALD W BHADY 
Sen lor \' iet' 
Administration 
Mr. Robert Leidigh 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
P. O. Box 807 
1100 K Street 
Sacramento, CA 95815 
Dear Bob: 
5.'\:\TA 
flARflMIA 
• 
SAI'TA 
CRCZ 
OFFICE OF THE PRESIDE'\T 
BEHKELE'( L\UFOH,\IA 
March 14, 1985 
As we discussed on March 13, there is a typographical error in Appendix A 
of the University's approved 1985 Conflict of Interest Code. The disclosure 
category for the Senior Vice Chancellor-Administration, San Francisco Campus 
(page 15 of the printed Code) is shown as 1, and it should have been 4 and 
17. All Vice Chancellors-Administration have been designated as 4 and 17 
categories in past Codes, and with the exception of the San Francisco Vice 
Chancellor are so indicated in our 1985 Code. 
Appendix A was originally sent to you for review on November 15, 1984, and 
the 4 and 17 categories were correctly listed for the San Francisco position. 
Appendix A, with only a revised order of designated officials, was again sent 
to you on November 21, 1984, and this is the copy on which the typographical 
error was made. 
Following our discussion, I have informally advised the Senior Vice Chancellor-
Administration at San Francisco to complete his statement of economic interest 
using the originally intended disclosure categories of 4 and 17. By this letter, 
I am asking you for a formal advice letter to officially correct the error. 
am sorry this happened, and, as always, appreciate your helpful assistance. 
cc: 
Chancellor-Administration Suelzle 
Sincerely, 
Aftot/E. Crooks 
Universitywide Conflict 
of Interest Coordinator 
San Francisco Conflict of Interest Coordinator Cowan 
Assistant Vice President Stover

sections.conclusion: This letter will formally confirm my previous telephone advice to the effect that you may treat the disclosure category for the Senior Vice Chancellor-Administration, San Francisco Campus as being 4 and 17, rather than 1. The assignment of category 1 was a clerical error when your office rearranged the order of designated officials and resulted in one campus' official being treated differently than like officials on all other campuses. This would be inappropriate. Therefore we will treat the category as being 4 and 17 for this annual report and will correct the clerical error when the Code is revised next fall.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether the disclosure category for the Senior Vice Chancellor-Administration at UC San Francisco should be corrected from category 1 to categories 4 and 17 due to a clerical error in the Conflict of Interest Code.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: FPPC confirms that the Senior Vice Chancellor-Administration at San Francisco Campus should use disclosure categories 4 and 17 rather than 1, as the latter was a clerical error. The correction will be applied for the current annual report and formally corrected in the Code revision scheduled for fall.

No formal Q/A section headers present, but the structure is clear with the request preceding the formal advice response.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether the disclosure category for the Senior Vice Chancellor-Administration at UC San Francisco should be corrected from category 1 to categories 4 and 17 due to a clerical error in the Conflict of Interest Code.

CONCLUSION: This letter will formally confirm my previous telephone advice to the effect that you may treat the disclosure category for the Senior Vice Chancellor-Administration, San Francisco Campus as being 4 and 17, rather than 1. The assignment of category 1 was a clerical error when your office rearranged the order of designated officials and resulted in one campus' official being treated differently than like officials on all other campuses. This would be inappropriate. Therefore we will treat the category as being 4 and 17 for this annual report and will correct the clerical error when the Code is revised next fall.

The assignment of category 1 was a clerical error when your office rearranged the order of designated officials and resulted in one campus' official being treated differently than like officials on all other campuses. embedding.summary: UC Berkeley's Conflict of Interest Coordinator requested formal advice regarding a clerical error in the 1985 Conflict of Interest Code that incorrectly assigned disclosure category 1 to the Senior Vice Chancellor-Administration at the San Francisco Campus instead of categories 4 and 17. FPPC Counsel Robert Leidigh confirmed the correction and authorized use of the proper categories for the current annual report.
